Scenarios:

Scenarios:

- workflow_without_auto_shows_intent_prompts_normally

## Artifact Store

### Change Creation

Scenarios:
- Successful change creation
- Slug strips stop words and special characters
- Duplicate change rejected
- Base versions recorded

### Change Listing

Scenarios:
- Lists active changes
- Empty list when no changes

### Artifact Status Tracking

Scenarios:
- Mark artifact complete
- Mark next artifact ready advances current_artifact

### Artifact File I/O

Scenarios:
- Write and read artifact
- Existence check

### Change Archival

Scenarios:
- Archive moves directory

### Change Abandonment

Scenarios:
- Abandon appends suffix

## ConfigLoader — Specification

## Context Engine — Specification

## custom-claude-statusline-conte

### Statusline script stdin contract

Scenarios:
- Valid stdin with both fields present
- Empty stdin treated as empty payload
- Malformed JSON on stdin treated as empty payload
- Stdin contains valid JSON but fields are wrong type

### Context window resolution

Scenarios:
- Model id contains [1m] substring
- Model id present but does not contain [1m]
- Model field absent
- Model field contains [1m] as part of a longer string

### Context utilization calculation

Scenarios:
- Transcript present with assistant usage entry
- Transcript present but no assistant usage entry found
- Transcript file does not exist
- Most recent assistant usage entry is used

### Metta artifact resolution

Scenarios:
- metta status returns active artifact
- metta status returns no active change
- metta binary not found on PATH
- metta status --json times out

### Output format

Scenarios:
- Full output with artifact and context percentage
- Output with idle artifact and no context percentage
- Unrecoverable error fallback
- Exit code is always 0

### Deterministic change-slug coloring

Scenarios:
- Active change slug produces ANSI-wrapped label
- Same slug produces same color on repeated runs
- Idle artifact is not colored
- Unknown fallback is not colored

### Install auto-registration

Scenarios:
- Fresh install writes script and updates settings
- Re-run install is a no-op
- Existing unrelated keys in settings.json are preserved
- User-authored statusLine pointing at a different path is not overwritten

### Install failure isolation

Scenarios:
- Script copy fails due to permissions error
- settings.json is unparseable JSON
- Failure does not suppress guard hook installation
- Warning message identifies the failed component

### Template build copy

Scenarios:
- Build copies statusline.mjs to dist
- Copied file is executable
- Build succeeds when statusline source directory is empty

### Statusline script stdin contract

Scenarios:
- Valid stdin with both fields present
- Empty stdin treated as empty payload
- Malformed JSON on stdin treated as empty payload
- Stdin contains valid JSON but fields are wrong type

### Context window resolution

Scenarios:
- Model id contains [1m] substring
- Model id present but does not contain [1m]
- Model field absent
- Model field contains [1m] as part of a longer string

### Context utilization calculation

Scenarios:
- Transcript present with assistant usage entry
- Transcript present but no assistant usage entry found
- Transcript file does not exist
- Most recent assistant usage entry is used

### Metta artifact resolution

Scenarios:
- metta status returns active artifact
- metta status returns no active change
- metta binary not found on PATH
- metta status --json times out

### Output format

Scenarios:
- Full output with artifact and context percentage
- Output with idle artifact and no context percentage
- Unrecoverable error fallback
- Exit code is always 0

### Deterministic change-slug coloring

Scenarios:
- Active change slug produces ANSI-wrapped label
- Same slug produces same color on repeated runs
- Idle artifact is not colored
- Unknown fallback is not colored

### Install auto-registration

Scenarios:
- Fresh install writes script and updates settings
- Re-run install is a no-op
- Existing unrelated keys in settings.json are preserved
- User-authored statusLine pointing at a different path is not overwritten

### Install failure isolation

Scenarios:
- Script copy fails due to permissions error
- settings.json is unparseable JSON
- Failure does not suppress guard hook installation
- Warning message identifies the failed component

### Template build copy

Scenarios:
- Build copies statusline.mjs to dist
- Copied file is executable
- Build succeeds when statusline source directory is empty

## Execution Engine Specification

## Finalize and Ship

### Spec Delta Merge

Scenarios:
- New capability created from ADDED delta
- Conflict detected on modified base
- Clean merge when base hash matches
- MODIFIED delta replaces requirement text
- RENAMED delta replaces old requirement with new name
- Dry-run does not write files
- No spec.md returns clean immediately

### Finalizer Orchestration

Scenarios:
- Successful finalize archives the change
- Finalize aborts on spec conflict
- Dry-run leaves change active
- gates.yaml written to archive

### Merge Safety Pipeline

Scenarios:
- Successful merge
- Dry-run skips git writes
- Conflict detected
- Base drift does not block merge

## fix-finalize-stage-should-auto-update-docs-changelog-md

### Default `docs` block in `ProjectConfigSchema`

Scenarios:
- empty config object yields populated docs defaults
- config with only `project` block yields populated docs defaults
- explicit partial `docs` block keeps overrides and fills missing fields
- explicit `generate_on: manual` is preserved

### Finalizer doc-generation guard

Scenarios:
- finalize regenerates docs when config has no `docs:` block
- finalize skips doc generation when `generate_on: manual`
- finalize swallows doc-generation errors

### Original docs-generate spec compliance

Scenarios:
- absent docs block produces all four doc types on finalize

### No changes to `DocGenerator`, `DocsConfigSchema` inner defaults, or finalizer flow ordering

Scenarios:
- scope discipline — only two source files changed

## fix-gate-infrastructure-bundle

### Gate execution MUST honor all three `on_failure` policies uniformly

Scenarios:
- retry_once retries on first fail
- retry_once does not retry on initial pass
- continue_with_warning downgrades fail to warn
- stop on fail causes remaining gates to be skipped
- stop does not affect gates earlier in the batch

### Gate `warn` status MUST be treated as pass across CLI commands

Scenarios:
- verify exits 0 when a gate returns warn
- verify exits non-zero when any gate returns fail
- finalize accepts warn results

### Workflow YAMLs MUST NOT reference unimplemented gates

Scenarios:
- standard.yaml does not reference unimplemented gates
- full.yaml does not reference unimplemented gates
- quick.yaml does not reference unimplemented gates

### GateRegistry tests MUST cover each policy branch

Scenarios:
- test suite asserts each on_failure branch

## fix-gate-runner-process-group-kill-timeout-scope-gates

### gate-runner-kills-process-group-on-timeout

Scenarios:
- timeout reaps grandchild processes
- clean completion does not fire PGID kill

### gate-runner-accepts-plain-string-command

Scenarios:
- existing gate YAML files run unchanged

### retry-once-kills-prior-pgid-before-retry

Scenarios:
- retry-once produces no lingering processes from either run

### finalizer-runs-only-workflow-declared-gates

Scenarios:
- quick workflow finalize skips stories-valid
- standard workflow finalize includes stories-valid

### quick-and-auto-archive-change-directory

Scenarios:
- finalize removes the changes directory
- finalize populates the archive directory

## fix-issue-full-workflow-refere

### full-workflow-artifact-templates

Scenarios:
- domain-research template load succeeds
- architecture template load succeeds
- ux-spec template load succeeds
- full workflow propose + instructions round-trip
- build pipeline copies the new templates

## fix-issue-stories-parser-multi

### stories-parser-tolerates-compact-format

Scenarios:
- compact stories.md parses all fields
- spaced stories.md (existing format) still works
- mixed format works

## fix-metta-guard-bash-allows-ai-orchestrators-bypass-skill

### Guard hook fails closed on high-value skill-enforced subcommands

Scenarios:
- Direct `metta issue` call is blocked and names the skill
- Inline-prefix `METTA_SKILL=1 metta propose` is blocked and names the skill

### Guard hook preserves inline-bypass for non-skill-enforced subcommands

Scenarios:
- Inline-prefix `METTA_SKILL=1 metta complete` is allowed when `complete` is enforced only via env-var path

### Guard hook appends every blocked or bypass-detected attempt to `.metta/logs/guard-bypass.log`

Scenarios:
- Blocked skill-enforced call appends one JSON line with verdict `block`
- Non-enforced inline-bypass call appends one JSON line with verdict `allow_with_bypass`

### Read-only subcommands are unaffected

Scenarios:
- `metta status` exits 0 with no audit-log entry
- `metta issues list` exits 0 with no audit-log entry

### Templates and deployed hook stay byte-identical

Scenarios:
- `diff -q` reports no difference between source and deployed hook
- Both test describe blocks assert the new fail-closed behavior

### Skills using inline METTA_SKILL=1 for enforced subcommands are updated to use an alternative dispatch

Scenarios:
- `/metta-issue` end-to-end still writes an issue file after skill dispatch is migrated

## fix-metta-next-gap-detect-unme

### next-skill-runs-ship

Scenarios:
- skill advances from ship response

### next-command-advances-post-finalize

Scenarios:
- next after finalize on a metta branch
- next on main with no active changes
- next on a metta branch with no unmerged commits
- next when main branch is missing

### next-skill-runs-ship

Scenarios:
- skill advances from ship response

## harden-metta-config-yaml-lifecycle-across-three-related-bugs

### Shared setProjectField config-writer helper

Scenarios:
- idempotent re-write produces no diff
- comment above mutated key is preserved

### metta install writes stacks via the shared helper

Scenarios:
- re-run idempotency — single stacks key after two installs
- three pre-existing duplicate stacks lines become one after install

### config-loader hard-fails on YAML parse errors

Scenarios:
- corrupt config blocks metta status with actionable message
- metta doctor is not blocked by the same corrupt config

### metta doctor --fix repairs duplicate-keys and schema-invalid config

Scenarios:
- three duplicate stacks lines collapsed to one with auto-commit
- schema-invalid key is dropped with a reported line

### ProjectConfigSchema carries a verification section

Scenarios:
- valid verification block is accepted by Zod
- invalid strategy enum value is rejected with a field-level error

### /metta-init Round 4 captures verification strategy

Scenarios:
- selecting tmux_tui with instructions writes verification block
- the two SKILL.md copies are byte-identical after the update

### metta instructions verification injects strategy into agent context

Scenarios:
- both fields populated when config has a full verification block
- both fields are null when verification block is absent

### verifier agents error on missing verification.strategy in non-default projects

Scenarios:
- legacy project config triggers a hard error with copy-pasteable remediation
- first-run verification with no changes defaults to tests_only with informational note

## metta-issue-metta-backlog-slas

### issue-slash-command

Scenarios:
- issue skill template exists and invokes the right CLI
- issue skill is deployed by install

### backlog-slash-command

Scenarios:
- backlog skill template exists and covers all subcommands
- backlog skill is deployed by install

## Schemas Specification

## skill-template-consistency-enforcement

### BashHookBlocksMutatingCommands

Scenarios:
- issue command blocked without bypass
- propose command blocked without bypass
- backlog subcommand blocked
- changes abandon blocked

### BashHookPassesReadOnlyCommands

Scenarios:
- status passes unconditionally
- all listed read-only commands pass
- unknown metta subcommand is not silently allowed

### BashHookBypassesWithMettaSkillEnv

Scenarios:
- skill bypass allows blocked command
- bypass applies to all mutating commands
- bypass does not require the command to be on the read-only list

### BashHookSkipsNonBashEvents

Scenarios:
- Edit tool event passes through
- Write tool event passes through

### BashHookEmergencyBypass

Scenarios:
- settings.local.json disable suppresses the hook
- local bypass does not affect settings.json
- removing the local bypass re-enables the hook

### HumanTerminalUsageUnaffected

Scenarios:
- terminal propose completes normally
- all blocklisted commands run normally at the terminal

### InstallRegistersHook

Scenarios:
- fresh install writes both hook entries
- idempotent re-install does not duplicate entries
- hook file is present after install

### ReviewFanOutPathsInTree

Scenarios:
- review files land in the change directory
- no review artifacts written to /tmp
- SKILL.md prose forbids /tmp for review

### VerifyFanOutPathsInTree

Scenarios:
- verify files land in the change directory
- no verify artifacts written to /tmp
- SKILL.md prose forbids /tmp for verify

### ByteIdenticalSkillMirrors

Scenarios:
- SKILL.md mirror matches template after update
- hook mirror matches template
- skill-discovery-loop test passes
- drift between mirrors fails CI

## spec-branch-safety-guard-metta-issue-metta-backlog-state

### assert-on-main-branch-helper

Scenarios:
- guard passes on main
- guard throws on feature branch without override
- override matching current branch passes
- override NOT matching current branch still throws
- non-git project passes silently

### issue-and-backlog-commands-use-branch-guard

Scenarios:
- metta issue blocks on feature branch
- metta backlog add blocks on feature branch
- metta backlog done blocks on feature branch
- --on-branch override allows write
- main branch path unchanged

## spec-metta-backlog-description-flag-whitelist-spec-issues

### backlog-add-accepts-description-flag

Scenarios:
- --description populates the body
- flag absent — backward-compatible behavior

### guard-hook-whitelists-issue-and-backlog-dirs

Scenarios:
- issue file Edit allowed without active change
- backlog file Edit allowed without active change
- non-whitelisted path still blocked

### metta-backlog-skill-uses-description-flag

Scenarios:
- skill template references --description
- post-add Edit workaround removed
- deployed mirror byte-identical

## Spec Model

### Spec Markdown Parsing

Scenarios:
- Full spec parsed
- MAY keyword extracted
- Empty spec handled

### Requirement Content Hashing

Scenarios:
- Stable hashes
- Different content produces different hash

### Delta Spec Parsing

Scenarios:
- Mixed delta operations parsed
- RENAMED operation parsed
- ADDED scenario prefix stripped

### Spec Lock Management

Scenarios:
- Lock created from spec
- Lock defaults to draft status and change source
- Custom source via createFromParsed
- Version incremented on update
- getBaseVersion returns null when no lock

## spec-project-local-gate-overrides-metta-gates-language

- project override replaces built-in

Scenarios:
- identical state across repeated loads

### docs-mention-override-path

Scenarios:
- docs grep hit

- each body now instructs the agent to return without committing

- executor body has no checkbox-flip instruction

## spec-stack-detection-at-install-gate-scaffolding-rust-python

### config-stacks-array-field

Scenarios:
- new config uses stacks array
- legacy string config is upgraded on load

### install-detects-stack-markers

Scenarios:
- Rust-only project detected
- Python-only via pyproject.toml
- Python-only via requirements.txt
- Multi-stack monorepo ordered by priority
- No markers

### install-scaffolds-gate-yamls

Scenarios:
- Rust primary → four rust gate files written
- Python primary → pass-through build
- Go primary → pass-through typecheck
- JS-only project creates no gate overrides
- Multi-stack adds comment block
- Scaffolding never overwrites

### install-stack-flag-override

Scenarios:
- --stack forces rust
- --stack multi-value
- --stack skip suppresses scaffolding
- Invalid --stack value exits with error

## spec:-centralize-slugify-utility-strip-non-ascii-truncate-at-word

### shared-to-slug-utility

Scenarios:
- basic lowercase and non-alphanumeric collapse
- em dash and non-ASCII stripping (US-1)
- word-boundary truncation (US-3)
- single-long-token hard-truncation
- stop-words filtered when opts.stopWords is provided
- empty input throws
- all-non-ASCII input throws

### spec-merger-uses-shared-to-slug

Scenarios:
- spec title with em dash produces clean folder name
- spec-merger imports toSlug, not a local helper

### all-store-sites-use-shared-to-slug

Scenarios:
- no legacy slugify pattern remains in src/
- artifact-store passes STOP_WORDS via opts
- each site imports toSlug from util/slug.js

### fix-issues-skill-uses-short-change-name

Scenarios:
- skill step 2 uses short slug form
- deployed mirror is byte-identical to template
- resulting change name avoids mid-word truncation

### word-boundary-truncation

Scenarios:
- truncation lands on word boundary
- result never exceeds maxLen
- no trailing hyphen after truncation
- single long token falls back to hard-truncation

Scenarios:
- skill file contains auto-commit documentation

## split-metta-install-metta-init

### init-command-drives-discovery

Scenarios:
- init after install in a brownfield project
- init on a greenfield project
- init before install is blocked
- init does not mutate the repository

- skill template references init command
- skill propagates to installed projects

- fresh install in a git repo

- install without a git repository
- human-readable install output points at init

## StateStore — Specification

## surface-time-token-budget-review-verifier-iteration-count

### Schema extension for per-artifact timing, tokens, and iteration counters

Scenarios:
- Legacy file without new fields validates cleanly
- New file round-trips all four fields
- Zod rejects invalid values

### `metta complete` stamps artifact `completed` timestamp

Scenarios:
- Complete stamps `completed`
- Complete preserves prior `started`
- Skipped artifact also records `completed`

### `metta instructions` stamps `started` and records token budget

Scenarios:
- Instructions sets `started` once
- Re-running instructions does not overwrite `started`
- Instructions records token budget
- Instrumentation failure does not abort the command

### `metta iteration` CLI command

Scenarios:
- First record creates the counter
- Subsequent record increments
- Independent counters
- Non-existent change errors clearly
- Single active change auto-selected

### `metta progress` surfaces new fields

Scenarios:
- JSON mode includes timings and tokens
- Human mode renders all three segments
- Suppress empty segments
- Git fallback for legacy timings

### `metta status` surfaces iteration counters and token totals

Scenarios:
- Status renders iteration line when counters set
- Status suppresses lines when fields absent

### Skill instructions record review and verify iterations

Scenarios:
- Each skill template invokes iteration record once per review round
- The prefix is always `METTA_SKILL=1`

## t8-post-merge-gate-re-run-afte

### post-merge-gate-execution

Scenarios:
- post-merge gates all pass
- any post-merge gate fails → rollback
- rollback failure surfaces clearly
- no gate registry injected

### ship-cli-passes-gate-registry

Scenarios:
- ship passes the loaded registry

## upgrade-metta-issue-skill-run-short-debugging-session-before

### Metta-issue skill performs root cause analysis before writing the ticket

Scenarios:
- Happy path — structured ticket written after RCA
- Interactive path — description not provided as argument

### Metta-issue skill supports --quick escape hatch

Scenarios:
- --quick skips RCA and writes a shallow ticket

### Metta-issue skill falls back to shallow log when RCA fails

Scenarios:
- RCA fails mid-session and fallback body is written

### Metta-issue skill writes a structured body with fixed section order

Scenarios:
- Full structured body validates section order and Evidence citation

### Metta issue CLI auto-detects piped stdin as body

Scenarios:
- Structured body piped via stdin becomes the issue body
- Interactive TTY stdin leaves behavior unchanged

### Issues-store parseIssue tolerates both freeform and structured bodies

Scenarios:
- Both freeform and structured bodies round-trip through metta issues show

### Metta-fix-issues skill surfaces structured issue sections at step 1

Scenarios:
- Structured sections displayed before fix planning begins

## user-story-layer-for-spec-format-(t5)

### Stories Artifact Phase in Standard Workflow

Scenarios:
- Standard workflow lists stories between intent and spec
- Spec artifact requires stories in standard workflow
- Quick workflow has no stories artifact

### Stories Document Format

Scenarios:
- Well-formed multi-story document is accepted
- Internal sentinel document is accepted
- Missing required field causes rejection

### Stories Zod Schema and Parser

Scenarios:
- Parser returns three stories from valid document
- Parser returns sentinel document
- Parser throws StoriesParseError on missing field
- Parser throws on duplicate US-N
- Parser throws on non-monotonic IDs

### validate-stories CLI Command

Scenarios:
- Valid stories exits 0
- Invalid stories exits 4 with error detail
- Missing change directory exits 4 not_found

### Spec Parser Fulfills Field

Scenarios:
- Requirement with Fulfills line exposes fulfills array
- Requirement without Fulfills line has empty array
- Malformed Fulfills produces warning not error

### Finalize Stories Gate

Scenarios:
- Clean change with valid stories passes gate
- Missing stories.md fails gate
- Broken Fulfills reference fails gate
- Drift warning does not block finalize

### metta-product Agent

Scenarios:
- Agent file exists with correct frontmatter
- Deployed copy is byte-identical to source template

### Stories Artifact Phase in Standard Workflow

Scenarios:
- Standard workflow lists stories between intent and spec
- Spec artifact requires stories in standard workflow
- Quick workflow has no stories artifact

### Stories Document Format

Scenarios:
- Well-formed multi-story document is accepted
- Internal sentinel document is accepted
- Missing required field causes rejection

### Stories Zod Schema and Parser

Scenarios:

- Parser throws on duplicate US-N
- Parser throws on non-monotonic IDs

Scenarios:
- Valid stories exits 0
- Invalid stories exits 4 with error detail
- Missing change directory exits 4 not_found

### Spec Parser Fulfills Field

Scenarios:

- Requirement without Fulfills line has empty array
- Malformed Fulfills produces warning not error

### Finalize Stories Gate

Scenarios:
- Clean change with valid stories passes gate
- Missing stories.md fails gate
- Broken Fulfills reference fails gate
- Drift warning does not block finalize

### metta-product Agent

Scenarios:
- Agent file exists with correct frontmatter
- Deployed copy is byte-identical to source template
